name: dev-servers
description: Start the backend (FastAPI/uvicorn) and frontend (Vite) development servers. Use when user mentions "start dev", "run servers", "launch app", "start the app", or needs to run the application locally.
capabilities: Start FastAPI backend server with uvicorn on port 8000
Start Vite frontend server on port 5173
Check for installed dependencies before starting servers
author: AppleLamps
securitySummary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate developer utility for starting local development servers. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns are false matches on 'uvicorn' text. 'Backtick execution' patterns are markdown code formatting in documentation. Hardcoded URLs are standard localhost addresses. Environment variable access is for configuration validation with explicit guardrails against key exposure.
valueStatement: Managing multiple development servers for a full-stack application can be error-prone and time-consuming. This skill simplifies launching FastAPI backend and Vite frontend development servers with proper dependency checking and port validation.
limitations: Only supports FastAPI backend and Vite frontend stack
Does not support custom port configurations
Requires pre-existing backend and frontend directories
Cannot manage servers running in Docker containers

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers working on Python and JavaScript applications
useCases.title: Full-stack Application Development
useCases.description: Launch both backend and frontend servers simultaneously for full-stack development workflow

useCases.targetUser: New team members onboarding to a project
useCases.title: Local Development Environment
useCases.description: Set up a complete local development environment with dependency verification

useCases.targetUser: QA engineers and developers running local tests
useCases.title: Application Testing
useCases.description: Quickly start development servers to test API changes and frontend updates

promptTemplates.title: Basic Server Start
promptTemplates.prompt: Start the dev servers for my application
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple request to launch all development servers

promptTemplates.title: Run Locally
promptTemplates.scenario: Request to run the application in development mode

promptTemplates.title: Launch Backend and Frontend
promptTemplates.scenario: Explicit request to start both server components

promptTemplates.title: Start Development Environment
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to start the development environment for my FastAPI and Vite project
promptTemplates.scenario: Detailed request with technology stack mentioned

bestPractices: Run backend and frontend servers in separate terminals to avoid port conflicts
Ensure OPENAI_API_KEY is set in backend/.env before starting
Check that ports 8000 and 5173 are available before launching
antiPatterns: Running the skill without verifying dependencies are installed first
Starting servers without checking if ports are already in use
Exposing or logging API keys during server startup

faq.question: What servers does this skill start?
faq.answer: The skill starts a FastAPI backend server using uvicorn on port 8000 and a Vite frontend server on port 5173.

faq.question: Does this skill install dependencies?
faq.answer: The skill checks for existing dependencies but does not automatically install them. It will prompt for confirmation before installing.

faq.question: Can I use custom ports?
faq.answer: This version does not support custom port configuration. The backend uses port 8000 and frontend uses port 5173.

faq.question: What if ports are already in use?
faq.answer: The skill includes a guardrail to warn if ports 8000 or 5173 are already in use by another process.

faq.question: Does this skill support Docker containers?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed for local development servers running directly on the host machine, not containers.

faq.question: What environment variables are required?
faq.answer: The skill checks for OPENAI_API_KEY in backend/.env. This is used for API configuration in the application.
